title: DirectiveArchetype
description: Schema for Directive Ascriptions — identity-level normative constraints (DNA 'D'). Defines the governance grammar: structure, modal, verb, qualifier, purpose. Extensible — tenant-defined archetypes MAY extend Directive via allOf to add domain-specific vocabulary while inheriting the governance grammar. DM input = DM output (1:1).

structure.description: ID reference to the authoring Structure that issues the Directive.

modal.description: Deontic obligation strength. MUST/MUST_NOT = hard constraint (violations = errors). SHOULD/SHOULD_NOT = soft constraint (violations = warnings). MAY = permitted (informational).

verb.description: Governance action expressing the governance direction on the qualifier Archetype's viability dimension.

qualifier.description: ID reference to the Archetype that defines the viability dimension being governed. Norms operationalizing this Directive MUST reference this same Archetype (or a descendant) in their guard/predicate CEL expressions.

purpose.description: ID reference to the purposed Structure whose purpose is governed by this Directive.
